**Condition:** When communicating with providers **or** consumers implementing API **v3.0.2 or earlier**, a sender implementing API **v3.0.3 or later MUST NOT** use `addressLines` as the only property to identify the location. Recipients implementing earlier versions **MAY ignore** this property.
Item description:
One line of an unstructured `Address`.

Path: IssuanceRequest / document / transports / placeOfReceipt / facility
Description: Facility related to the location.

Path: IssuanceRequest / document / transports / placeOfReceipt / facility / facilityCode
Description:
The code used for identifying the specific facility. This code does not include the UN Location Code.
The definition of the code depends on the `facilityCodeListProvider`. As code list providers maintain multiple codeLists the following codeList is used:
- `SMDG` (the codeList used is the [SMDG Terminal Code List](https://smdg.org/documents/smdg-code-lists/))
- `BIC` (the codeList used is the [BIC Facility Codes](https://www.bic-code.org/facility-codes/))

Path: IssuanceRequest / document / transports / placeOfReceipt / facility / facilityCodeListProvider
Description:
The provider used for identifying the facility Code. Some facility codes are only defined in combination with an `UN Location Code`
- `BIC` (Requires a UN Location Code)
- `SMDG` (Requires a UN Location Code)

Path: IssuanceRequest / document / transports / placeOfReceipt / facilityName
Description: The name of the facility.

Path: IssuanceRequest / document / transports / placeOfReceipt / geoCoordinate
Description: Geographic coordinates of the location.

Path: IssuanceRequest / document / transports / placeOfReceipt / geoCoordinate / latitude
Description: Latitude expressed as decimal degrees using the ISO 6709 data interchange numeric format.

Path: IssuanceRequest / document / transports / placeOfReceipt / geoCoordinate / longitude
Description: Longitude expressed as decimal degrees using the ISO 6709 data interchange numeric format.

Path: IssuanceRequest / document / transports / placeOfReceipt / locationName
Description: The name of the location.

Path: IssuanceRequest / document / transports / placeOfReceipt / UNLocationCode
Description:
The UN Location code specifying where the place is located. The pattern used must be

- 2 characters for the country code using [ISO 3166-1 alpha-2](https://www.iso.org/obp/ui/#iso:pub:PUB500001:en)
- 3 characters to code a location within that country. Letters A-Z and numbers from 2-9 can be used

More info can be found here: [UN/LOCODE](https://unece.org/trade/cefact/UNLOCODE-Download)

Path: IssuanceRequest / document / transports / plannedArrivalDate
Description: The planned date of arrival.

Path: IssuanceRequest / document / transports / plannedDepartureDate
Description: The planned date of departure.

Path: IssuanceRequest / document / transports / portOfDischarge
Description: The port where the cargo is discharged from the vessel.

Path: IssuanceRequest / document / transports / portOfDischarge / city
Description: City and country where the port is located.

Path: IssuanceRequest / document / transports / portOfDischarge / city / city
Description: The name of the city.

Path: IssuanceRequest / document / transports / portOfDischarge / city / countryCode
Description:
The 2 characters for the country code using [ISO 3166-1 alpha-2](https://www.iso.org/obp/ui/#iso:pub:PUB500001:en)

**Note:** In case the `countryCode` is unknown it is possible to use the code `ZZ`.

Path: IssuanceRequest / document / transports / portOfDischarge / city / stateRegion
Description: The name of the state/region.

Path: IssuanceRequest / document / transports / portOfDischarge / locationName
Description: The name of the location.

Path: IssuanceRequest / document / transports / portOfDischarge / UNLocationCode
Description:
The UN Location code specifying where the place is located. The pattern used must be

- 2 characters for the country code using [ISO 3166-1 alpha-2](https://www.iso.org/obp/ui/#iso:pub:PUB500001:en)
- 3 characters to code a location within that country. Letters A-Z and numbers from 2-9 can be used

More info can be found here: [UN/LOCODE](https://unece.org/trade/cefact/UNLOCODE-Download)

Path: IssuanceRequest / document / transports / portOfLoading
Description: The port where the cargo is loaded onto the vessel.

Path: IssuanceRequest / document / transports / portOfLoading / city
Description: City and country where the port is located.

Path: IssuanceRequest / document / transports / portOfLoading / city / city
Description: The name of the city.

Path: IssuanceRequest / document / transports / portOfLoading / city / countryCode
Description:
The 2 characters for the country code using [ISO 3166-1 alpha-2](https://www.iso.org/obp/ui/#iso:pub:PUB500001:en)

**Note:** In case the `countryCode` is unknown it is possible to use the code `ZZ`.

Path: IssuanceRequest / document / transports / portOfLoading / city / stateRegion
Description: The name of the state/region.

Path: IssuanceRequest / document / transports / portOfLoading / locationName
Description: The name of the location.

Path: IssuanceRequest / document / transports / portOfLoading / UNLocationCode
Description:
The UN Location code specifying where the place is located. The pattern used must be

- 2 characters for the country code using [ISO 3166-1 alpha-2](https://www.iso.org/obp/ui/#iso:pub:PUB500001:en)
- 3 characters to code a location within that country. Letters A-Z and numbers from 2-9 can be used

More info can be found here: [UN/LOCODE](https://unece.org/trade/cefact/UNLOCODE-Download)

Path: IssuanceRequest / document / transports / preCarriageBy
Description:
Mode of transportation for pre-carriage when transport to the port of loading is organized by the carrier. If this attributes is populated, then a Place of Receipt must also be defined. The currently supported values include:
- `VESSEL` (Vessel)
- `RAIL` (Rail)
- `TRUCK` (Truck)
- `BARGE` (Barge)
- `MULTIMODAL` (if multiple modes are used)

Path: IssuanceRequest / document / transports / vesselVoyages
Description: Allow the possibility to include multiple vessels/voyages in the `Transport Document` (e.g. the first sea going vessel and the mother vessel). At least one is mandatory to provide.

Path: IssuanceRequest / document / transports / vesselVoyages / carrierExportVoyageNumber
Description: The identifier of an export voyage. The carrier-specific identifier of the export Voyage.

Path: IssuanceRequest / document / transports / vesselVoyages / role
Description:
Identifies the role of the vessel voyage within the Transport Document. Possible values are:
- `FIRST_SEA_GOING` (First sea-going vessel)
- `MOTHER` (Mother vessel)

Path: IssuanceRequest / document / transports / vesselVoyages / universalExportVoyageReference
Description:
A global unique voyage reference for the export Voyage, as per DCSA standard, agreed by VSA partners for the voyage. The voyage reference must match the regular expression pattern: `\d{2}[0-9A-Z]{2}[NEWSR]`
- `2 digits` for the year
- `2 alphanumeric characters` for the sequence number of the voyage
- `1 character` for the direction/haul (`N`orth, `E`ast, `W`est, `S`outh or `R`oundtrip).

Path: IssuanceRequest / document / transports / vesselVoyages / vesselName
Description: The name of the first sea going Vessel on board which the cargo is loaded or intended to be loaded

Path: IssuanceRequest / document / utilizedTransportEquipments
Description: A list of `Utilized Transport Equipments` describing the equipment being used.

Path: IssuanceRequest / document / utilizedTransportEquipments / activeReeferSettings
Description: Active reefer settings applied to this equipment.

Path: IssuanceRequest / document / utilizedTransportEquipments / activeReeferSettings / airExchangeSetpoint
Description: Target value for the air exchange rate which is the rate at which outdoor air replaces indoor air within a Reefer container

Path: IssuanceRequest / document / utilizedTransportEquipments / activeReeferSettings / airExchangeUnit
Description:
The unit for `airExchange` in metrics- or imperial- units per hour
- `MQH` (Cubic metre per hour)
- `FQH` (Cubic foot per hour)

**Condition:** Mandatory if `airExchange` is provided. If `airExchange` is not provided, this field must be empty.

Path: IssuanceRequest / document / utilizedTransportEquipments / activeReeferSettings / co2Setpoint
Description: The percentage of the controlled atmosphere CO<sub>2</sub> target value

Path: IssuanceRequest / document / utilizedTransportEquipments / activeReeferSettings / humiditySetpoint
Description: The percentage of the controlled atmosphere humidity target value

Path: IssuanceRequest / document / utilizedTransportEquipments / activeReeferSettings / isBulbMode
Description: Is special container setting for handling flower bulbs active

Path: IssuanceRequest / document / utilizedTransportEquipments / activeReeferSettings / isColdTreatmentRequired
Description: Indicator whether cargo requires cold treatment prior to loading at origin or during transit, but prior arrival at POD

Path: IssuanceRequest / document / utilizedTransportEquipments / activeReeferSettings / isControlledAtmosphereRequired
Description: Indicator of whether cargo requires Controlled Atmosphere.

Path: IssuanceRequest / document / utilizedTransportEquipments / activeReeferSettings / isDrainholesOpen
Description: Is drain holes open on the container

Path: IssuanceRequest / document / utilizedTransportEquipments / activeReeferSettings / isVentilationOpen
Description: If `true` the ventilation orifice is `Open` - if `false` the ventilation orifice is `closed`

Path: IssuanceRequest / document / utilizedTransportEquipments / activeReeferSettings / o2Setpoint
Description: The percentage of the controlled atmosphere O<sub>2</sub> target value

Path: IssuanceRequest / document / utilizedTransportEquipments / activeReeferSettings / temperatureSetpoint
Description: Target value of the temperature for the Reefer based on the cargo requirement.

Path: IssuanceRequest / document / utilizedTransportEquipments / activeReeferSettings / temperatureUnit
Description:
The unit for temperature in Celsius or Fahrenheit

- `CEL` (Celsius)
- `FAH` (Fahrenheit)

**Condition:** Mandatory if `temperatureSetpoint` is provided. If `temperatureSetpoint` is not provided, this field must be empty.

Path: IssuanceRequest / document / utilizedTransportEquipments / customsReferences
Description: A list of `Customs references`

Path: IssuanceRequest / document / utilizedTransportEquipments / customsReferences / countryCode
Description:
The 2 characters for the country code using [ISO 3166-1 alpha-2](https://www.iso.org/obp/ui/#iso:pub:PUB500001:en)

**Note:** In case the `countryCode` is unknown it is possible to use the code `ZZ`.

Path: IssuanceRequest / document / utilizedTransportEquipments / customsReferences / type
Description: The reference type code as defined in the relevant customs jurisdiction.

Path: IssuanceRequest / document / utilizedTransportEquipments / customsReferences / values
Description:
Item description:
The value of the `customsReference`

Path: IssuanceRequest / document / utilizedTransportEquipments / equipment
Description: The container or transport equipment used.

Path: IssuanceRequest / document / utilizedTransportEquipments / equipment / equipmentReference
Description:
The unique identifier for the equipment, which should follow the BIC ISO Container Identification Number where possible.
According to [ISO 6346](https://www.iso.org/standard/83558.html), a container identification code consists of a 4-letter prefix and a 7-digit number (composed of a 3-letter owner code, a category identifier, a serial number, and a check-digit).

If a container does not comply with [ISO 6346](https://www.iso.org/standard/83558.html), it is suggested to follow [Recommendation #2: Containers with non-ISO identification](https://smdg.org/documents/smdg-recommendations) from SMDG.

Path: IssuanceRequest / document / utilizedTransportEquipments / equipment / ISOEquipmentCode
Description: Unique code for the different equipment size and type used to transport commodities. The code can refer to one of ISO size type (e.g. 22G1) or ISO type group (e.g. 22GP) following the [ISO 6346](https://www.iso.org/standard/83558.html) standard.

Path: IssuanceRequest / document / utilizedTransportEquipments / equipment / tareWeight
Description: The weight of an empty container (gross container weight).

Path: IssuanceRequest / document / utilizedTransportEquipments / equipment / tareWeight / unit
Description:
The unit of measure which can be expressed in imperial or metric terms
- `KGM` (Kilograms)
- `LBR` (Pounds)

Path: IssuanceRequest / document / utilizedTransportEquipments / equipment / tareWeight / value
Description: The weight of an empty container (gross container weight).

Path: IssuanceRequest / document / utilizedTransportEquipments / isNonOperatingReefer
Description:
If the equipment is a Reefer Container then setting this attribute will indicate that the container should be treated as a `DRY` container.

**Condition:** Only applicable if `ISOEquipmentCode` shows a Reefer type.

Path: IssuanceRequest / document / utilizedTransportEquipments / isShipperOwned
Description: Indicates whether the container is shipper owned (SOC).

Path: IssuanceRequest / document / utilizedTransportEquipments / references
Description: A list of `References`

Path: IssuanceRequest / document / utilizedTransportEquipments / references / type
Description:
The reference type codes defined by DCSA. Possible values are:
- `CR` (Customer's Reference)
- `AKG` (Vehicle Identification Number)

Path: IssuanceRequest / document / utilizedTransportEquipments / references / value
Description: The value of the reference.

Path: IssuanceRequest / document / utilizedTransportEquipments / seals
Description: A list of `Seals`

Path: IssuanceRequest / document / utilizedTransportEquipments / seals / number
Description: Identifies a seal affixed to the container.

Path: IssuanceRequest / document / utilizedTransportEquipments / seals / source
Description:
The source of the seal, namely who has affixed the seal.
- `CAR` (Carrier)
- `SHI` (Shipper)
- `VET` (Veterinary)
- `CUS` (Customs)

**Condition:** Seal source may be required depending on the type of commodity being shipped.

Path: IssuanceRequest / document / utilizedTransportEquipments / shippingMarks
Description:
A list of the `ShippingMarks` applicable to this `UtilizedTransportEquipment`

**Condition:** The order of the items in this array **MUST** be preserved as by the provider of the API.
Item description:
The identifying details of a package or the actual markings that appear on the package(s). This information is provided by the customer.

Path: IssuanceRequest / eBLVisualisationByCarrier
IssuanceRequest / eBLVisualisationByCarrier / content
Description: The actual contents of the visual rendering.

Path: IssuanceRequest / eBLVisualisationByCarrier / contentType
Description:
The `Media Type` of the content being transmitted as defined by [Iana](https://www.iana.org/assignments/media-types/media-types.xhtml). Can be left out if the content is `application/pdf` (PDF).

**Condition:** This property is mandatory to provide if it differs from `application/pdf`

Path: IssuanceRequest / eBLVisualisationByCarrier / name
Example: Carrier rendered copy of the EBL.pdf
Description: Name of the Supporting Document

Path: IssuanceRequest / issuanceManifestSignedContent
Description: JWS content with compact serialization according to [RFC 7515](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc7515#section-7.1). JWS-signed payload is defined in schema [IssuanceManifest](#/IssuanceManifest). This attribute is used to provide integrity of various parts of the payload that enable parties to validate whether a payload matches what the carrier issued originally. Accordingly, the payload is signed by the carrier or a party (typically an agent) acting on behalf of the carrier.

Path: IssuanceRequest / issueTo
Description: The party to whom the `Bill of Lading` must be issued.

Path: IssuanceRequest / issueTo / identifyingCodes
IssuanceRequest / issueTo / identifyingCodes / codeListName
Description:
The name of the code list, code generation mechanism or code authority for the `partyCode`. Example values could be:

- `DID` (Decentralized Identifier) for `codeListProvider` `W3C`
- `LEI` (Legal Entity Identifier) for `codeListProvider` `GLEIF`
- `DUNS` (Data Universal Numbering System) for `codeListProvider` `DNB`

Path: IssuanceRequest / issueTo / identifyingCodes / codeListProvider
Description:
A list of codes identifying a party. Possible values are:

- `WAVE` (Wave)
- `CARX` (CargoX)
- `ESSD` (EssDOCS - This value has been **deprecated**. Please use `IDT`)
- `IDT` (ICE Digital Trade)
- `BOLE` (Bolero)
- `EDOX` (EdoxOnline)
- `IQAX` (IQAX)
- `SECR` (Secro)
- `TRGO` (TradeGO)
- `ETEU` (eTEU)
- `TRAC` (Enigio trace:original)
- `BRIT` (BRITC eBL)
- `COVA` (Covantis)
- `ETIT` (e-title)
- `KTNE` (KTNET)
- `CRED` (Credore)
- `BLOC` (BlockPeer Technologies)
- `DOCU` (DocuTrade)
- `AEOT` (AEOTrade)
- `SGTD` (SGTraDex)
- `GSBN` (Global Shipping Business Network)
- `WISE` (WiseTech)
- `GLEIF` (Global Legal Entity Identifier Foundation)
- `W3C` (World Wide Web Consortium)
- `DNB` (Dun and Bradstreet)
- `FMC` (Federal Maritime Commission)
- `DCSA` (Digital Container Shipping Association)
- `ZZZ` (Mutually defined)

Path: IssuanceRequest / issueTo / identifyingCodes / partyCode
Description: Code to identify the party as provided by the code list provider

Path: IssuanceRequest / issueTo / partyName
Description: Name of the party.

The eBL platform of the transaction party.

- `SECR` (Secro)

- `ETIT` (e-title)
- `KTNE` (KTNET)
- `CRED` (Credore)

- `DOCU` (DocuTrade)
- `AEOT` (AEOTrade)
- `SGTD` (SGTraDex)

Path: IssuanceRequest / issueTo / taxLegalReferences
Description: A list of `Tax References` for a `Party`

Path: IssuanceRequest / issueTo / taxLegalReferences / countryCode
Description:
The 2 characters for the country code using [ISO 3166-1 alpha-2](https://www.iso.org/obp/ui/#iso:pub:PUB500001:en)

Path: IssuanceRequest / issueTo / taxLegalReferences / type
